Off Calendar Jun-08-2026 Continued To Aug-31-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT T-MOBILE USA, INC.'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE TRIAL

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Transferred to Department 206 on August 31, 2026, to be sent out to the assigned trial judge. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set Two), Special Interrogatories (Set Two), And Requests For Admission (Set Two); Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET TWO), SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET TWO), AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION (SET TWO); REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Appearances via Zoom:
Tom Xu of the Han Law Firm (949.236.6767) for Defendant T-Mobile USA, Inc.;
No other appearances.

There are no other appearances on this matter when called at 9:25 am. Mr. Xu presents arguments and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Raulito Balingit's motion to compel further responses to his set two form interrogatories, set two special Interrogatories, and set two requests for admission and for monetary sanctions is granted in large part.

Plaintiff seeks to compel further responses to form interrogatory 17.1, special interrogatories 74-75, 76-81, and 82-89, and requests for admission 36-54. All of these interrogatories and requests are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and most of T-Mobile's responses are incomplete and most of its objections lack merit. Plaintiff's meet and confer was reasonable.

T-Mobile represented that it will provide further responses to requests 52-54 and interrogatories 17.1, 76-81 and 82-89. T-Mobile shall provide those verified responses within 10 days of notice of entry of this order if it has not done so already.

T-Mobile's objections to requests 36 and 40-51 are overruled. T-Mobile's responses to those requests do not comply with CCP 2033.220. T-Mobile should have a copy of its dealer agreement and provide complete responses. within 10 days of notice of entry of this order.

T-Mobile's vague and ambiguous objections are sustained as to requests 37-39 because the cited provision (section 1.22) purportedly does not exist.

The motion is denied as to interrogatories 74-75. "None" is a proper response since T-Mobile has no documents related to the cited employees.

To the extent T-Mobile contends that any response implicates the attorney work-product and/or attorney client privilege, it shall produce a privilege log within 10 days of notice of entry of this order.

T-Mobile did not act with substantial justification. T-Mobile and/or its counsel shall pay $1,800 in reasonable sanctions to plaintiff no later than 10 court days from notice of entry of this order.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
